recent_calls.summary: Raphael called to report that his furnace is still leaking water after a recent repair visit, suspecting an issue with the A coil. The agent acknowledged the problem, confirmed contact details, and assured that the team would follow up promptly. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further questions.
The user requested to speak with a live representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called by mistake but shared that the issue with their fan was due to the speed being set too low, causing the coils to freeze and no cooling. The user plans to leave the fan off for a day to see if it improves and expressed gratitude for the service provided by Cole. The call ended amicably with no further assistance requested.
The user requested to speak with a live representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Jaunice, called to schedule a cooling service appointment. The agent attempted to gather details about the user's role and the service needed but had difficulty understanding some responses. The call ended with the agent transferring the user to a live representative for further assistance.
The call was very brief and disconnected quickly after reaching voicemail, with no meaningful conversation taking place.
The user requested to speak with someone, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to schedule a specific service related to their flat and mentioned American Home Shield. The agent tried to gather more details and requested the home warranty dispatch number, but the call ended abruptly before completion.
The user returned a call from Budget and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent facilitated the transfer without any issues.

The user, Ali from American Home Shield, called to inquire about an earlier appointment for a home warranty work order scheduled on June 24th. The agent explained that appointment adjustments for home warranty jobs follow a different process and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to get an update on a service appointment for Anita M. Muhammad at 708 Zions Hill Drive, St. Louis. The agent confirmed the appointment details and informed the user that technician Brooks was scheduled to arrive between 11 AM and 12 PM. Since the technician had not arrived after 12 PM, the agent transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called and asked to speak to Nicole. The agent attempted to connect the user to Nicole and successfully transferred the call to another agent.
The user requested to speak with someone, and the agent promptly initiated a transfer to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred without further interaction.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the call was promptly transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate number. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.

Sharon from American Home Shield escalations team called Budget to discuss a dispatch and requested to be transferred to a live person for assistance. The agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The call ended with a successful transfer.

Anita Muhammad called to check the arrival time for her heating and cooling service appointment. The agent confirmed the appointment with technician Brooks scheduled between 11 AM and 12 PM today. Anita had no further questions and ended the call positively.

The user called Budget and was initially unclear in their request, but eventually indicated they wanted to speak with a representative. The agent attempted to clarify the request and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called to speak with a representative and the agent transferred the call to a live agent successfully. The call lasted 17 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

The user, RT from American Home Shield, called to request moving an HVAC service appointment for an air conditioning unit earlier than the scheduled June 23rd date. The agent confirmed details including the correct customer name, address, and phone number, and assured that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact the customer soon. The user was satisfied with the call and provided positive feedback.

The user Abby from American Home Shield called Budget regarding a work order and provided a dispatch number. The agent Sophie confirmed the dispatch number and transferred the call to a live representative for further assistance.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Steven Lang, called to request a hot water heater repair and provided his contact details and service address. The agent informed him of the service fee and availability, noting that the earliest appointment might be on June 22, but the user preferred the earliest possible date. The call ended with the user seeking confirmation about rescheduling if same-day service was unavailable.

The user, Craig Simpson, called to request plumbing service for a main line backup at his home in Saint Louis, Missouri. The agent collected all necessary details and informed Craig of the earliest available service slots, which are on June 22nd. Craig confirmed the information provided by the agent.
The call was initiated by the user but ended abruptly after the agent asked for the service address ZIP code, with no further interaction.

The user called to schedule a technician to check her mom's air conditioning system, which is not working. The agent booked an appointment for Monday, June 22nd at noon and informed the user about the standard dispatch fee. The user expressed some dissatisfaction due to not knowing the exact price but confirmed the appointment details.

The user called to inquire about a service appointment but the agent was unable to find the appointment in the system. The user requested to be transferred to a live representative for further assistance, and the call was successfully transferred.
The user called and requested to speak with customer service. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called to schedule a service after missing a previous call from the company. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about their home warranty coverage and upgrade options, specifically for their air conditioning system. The agent provided detailed information about the current plan and upgrade benefits, then transferred the user to a live agent for further assistance with upgrade options. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user, Danielle, called Budget after receiving a call about scheduling an appointment for a home warranty service related to cooling. The agent began gathering details about the cooling issue but the call ended abruptly before completion.
The user requested to speak to customer service, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Pamela called to report receiving an email about a new service ticket, which she did not request since her last appointment. The agent confirmed the issue and assured Pamela that the team would look into it and follow up. The call ended with no further questions from Pamela.
The user called Budget and requested to speak with a live agent. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and transferred the call to another agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with Shannon Gregory, and the agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred to another number for further assistance.

Rose from American Home Shield called to schedule an appointment for an AC central electric recall for customer Dottie Mesterak. The agent confirmed details, including the issue with the fan not working and no cooling, and noted that the team will reach out to coordinate scheduling. Rose declined information about maintenance programs and gave positive feedback about the call experience.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

Jerry from American Home Shield called Sophie at Budget to request an update on a work order with dispatch number 54106149. Sophie asked for Jerry's full name and best contact number to document the request. The call ended shortly after without further resolution.

The user called to inquire about service availability in ZIP code 84124, but the agent informed them that service is not offered in that area. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The call ended with a successful transfer without further conversation.

The user called to inquire about mechanical inspections and requested to speak with someone regarding a specific address. The agent attempted to gather more details but ultimately transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Raymond Sanchez called to resolve an issue with his AC unit, which is not working and awaiting part authorization from Budget Heating and Cooling through HSA. The agent updated the request to reflect that HSA is waiting on Budget Heating and Cooling to send the report for part approval. Raymond declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests, and the call ended with a promise that someone would contact him soon.
The user called Budget and spoke briefly with Sophie, who identified the call as related to customer service. Sophie then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about the amount needed to make a payment. The agent informed the user that they do not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

Catherine from American Home Shield contacted Budget to request a detailed diagnosis update for a cooling issue at a property. The agent confirmed the dispatch number and contact details, and the user declined information about maintenance programs. The call ended positively with the user providing excellent feedback.
The user called to inquire about their HVAC system and wanted to speak with someone regarding it. The call ended abruptly after the user repeated their request and tried to get the agent's attention.
The user requested to speak with someone, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to inquire about financial aid eligibility and requested that their information be passed to the team for follow-up. They also reported a broken cooling system and expressed interest in scheduling a service appointment, though they were flexible with the timing. The agent confirmed the user's details and informed them of available appointment slots, but the user declined specific times and ended the call.
